subject: Product Management
title: Build Cheap Tests Before Expensive Products
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Build Cheap Tests Before Expensive Products.

hook.text: Quibi spent $1.75 billion before learning nobody wanted its product. A landing page test could have revealed that for $500.

buildup.text: The pattern repeats: Google Glass, Segway, Fire Phone — massive investment before validating core assumptions.

discovery.text: Every failed product had testable assumptions. 'Will people wear cameras on their face?' could have been tested with a prototype.

twist.text: Teams skip cheap tests because they feel beneath them. Building the real thing feels more productive than testing a mockup.

climax.text: The lesson from every product graveyard: the cost of a test is always less than the cost of building the wrong thing.

punchline.text: Test the assumption before building the product.

quiz.question: What lesson do most product failures share?
options: Core assumptions should have been tested cheaply before massive investment
Products always fail because of bad technology
Failure is always caused by insufficient funding
